senses.gloss: Hanafism, a certain school of jurisprudence
Hanafism, a certain school of jurisprudence; Hanafis

etymology.text:
Etymology tree
Arabic حَنِيف (ḥanīf)
Arabic ـِيّ (-iyy)
Proto-Afroasiatic *-t
Proto-Semitic *-at-
Arabic ـَة (-a)
Arabic ـِيَّة (-iyya)
Arabic حنفية
From حَنِيف (ḥanīf) + ـِيَّة (-iyya).

body.text: persons called in relation to the Imám Aboo-Haneefeh
[is its n. un.: and] signifies [also] one
who is of the religion of Abraham.
a vessel with a tap, for the purpose of ablution,
such as is often used in a private house; and
a tank with taps, for the same purpose,

in a mosque; because persons of the persuasion of Aboo-Haneefeh must perform the ablution preparatory to prayer with running water, or from a tank or the like at least ten cubits in breadth and the same in depth;] but this application is post-classical. (TA.)       -A3-
, (so in a MS. copy of the K,)
Certain swords, so called in relation to El-Ahnaf Ibn- Keys;
because he was the first who ordered to make them: by rule it should be
